metadata.title: SpaceX / SPCX IPO + cancelable AI-compute deals (Google, Anthropic)
metadata.supersedes: sec-filings.json spacex_s1_status (which correctly reported 'not yet filed' as of 2026-06-06; the S-1 became public 2026-05-20 and was confirmed via search 2026-06-07)
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (TechCrunch, Yahoo Finance, Reuters-sourced aggregators, VC Corner S-1 teardown). S-1 figures are from the prospectus; treat valuation/price as the offering range until priced.

entities.name: SpaceX
entities.notes: S-1 filed 2026-05-20, S-1/A 2026-06-01; Nasdaq/Nasdaq Texas SPCX; ~$135/share, 555.6M shares all-primary; $1.75T-$2T target. 2025 rev $18.67B (Starlink/connectivity ~$10-11.4B, launch ~$6.4B, Starshield ~$1.8B). 10.3M Starlink subs, 9,600+ sats, ~50% YoY growth.

edges.notes: MAJOR CORRECTION: Alphabet held ~6.11% of SpaceX (end-2025, revealed Apr-2026 filing); ~$100-122B at a $1.75-2T IPO (~5% diluted post xAI merger). This NON-cancelable equity stake DWARFS the ~$11B/yr compute deal and is a two-way contagion channel: a SpaceX valuation shock hits a CORE hyperscaler's (Google's) balance sheet directly.

SpaceX and xAI reportedly merged (SpaceX stake in xAI diluted Google's SpaceX % to ~5%). Fuses an AI-lab into SpaceX's cap table - pulls the AI core into SpaceX structurally, beyond the cancelable compute deals.

GENUINE external cash flow (broadband subs + govt launch + Starshield). This is why SpaceX is fundamentally less bubble-exposed than the AI labs - it sells a real service to real non-cluster customers.
$75B all-primary raise at ~$1.75T (~95-107x 2025 sales). Public-market capital injection; valuation embeds AI-compute optionality (below).

$920M/MONTH Oct 2026-Jun 2029 (~$30B total) for ~110,000 NVIDIA GPUs+CPUs. CANCELABLE: either party 90 days' notice after Dec 31 2026; Google may terminate if SpaceX misses GPU delivery by Sep 30 2026. Signed DAYS before IPO pricing = revenue-padding with contingent contracts.

$1.25B/MONTH through May 2029 (~$45B) for SpaceX's 'Colossus' compute infra (xAI-linked). CANCELABLE: either party 90 days' notice after Dec 31 2026. Pulls SpaceX into the circular core via Anthropic->SpaceX->NVIDIA->Anthropic - but ONLY through a cancelable edge.

To service the Google/Anthropic deals SpaceX provisions ~110k+ NVIDIA GPUs -> SpaceX becomes a NVIDIA customer AND a compute RESELLER. This is the edge that closes the cancelable cycle into the core SCC.
SpaceX reportedly invested ~$2B in xAI; intra-Musk-empire capital. 'Colossus' is xAI's supercomputer - SpaceX reselling xAI/NVIDIA compute is the Musk-cluster's own mini-loop.

assessment: SpaceX has TWO revenue layers: (1) a DURABLE, exogenous base (Starlink/launch/Starshield, ~$18.7B 2025, ~50% growth, real non-cluster customers) and (2) a CONTINGENT, cancelable AI-compute-reselling layer (~$26B/yr run-rate from Google+Anthropic, all terminable after Dec 31 2026). Layer 1 makes SpaceX fundamentally less bubble-exposed than the AI labs (validates the separability thesis). Layer 2 shows the bubble REACHING for SpaceX: cancelable AI-compute deals signed days before IPO to pad the prospectus, conditionally inserting SpaceX into the NVIDIA circular core. The cancelability is the tell - real demand does not need a 90-day exit.
